cards.front: Who led the Chittagong Armory Raid and when?
cards.back: Surya Sen (Masterda) — 18 April 1930. He was hanged in 1934.

cards.front: Khudiram Bose was hanged in which year and at what age?
cards.back: 1908, at age 18. He was one of the youngest martyrs of the Indian freedom struggle. (Muzaffarpur bomb case)

cards.front: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year and between whom?
cards.back: 1757 — between Robert Clive (EIC) and Nawab Siraj-ud-Daulah. Established British dominance in Bengal.

cards.front: Who gave the slogan 'Do or Die' and during which movement?
cards.back: Mahatma Gandhi — during the Quit India Movement, 8 August 1942, Bombay (Gowalia Tank).

cards.front: The Permanent Settlement (1793) was introduced by whom?
cards.back: Lord Cornwallis. It fixed land revenue permanently with Zamindars in Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa.

cards.front: When was the Partition of Bengal and who ordered it?
cards.back: 1905 — Lord Curzon. Annulled in 1911 when capital was shifted from Calcutta to Delhi.

cards.front: Rabindranath Tagore won the Nobel Prize in which year and for which work?
cards.back: 1913 — for Gitanjali. He was the first Asian to win a Nobel Prize.

cards.front: Why did Tagore renounce his Knighthood?
cards.back: To protest the Jallianwala Bagh massacre of 13 April 1919, ordered by General Dyer in Amritsar.

cards.front: What was the Dandi March and when did it start?
cards.back: Gandhi's 241-mile march starting 12 March 1930 to Dandi (Gujarat) to break the Salt Law — launching Civil Disobedience Movement.

cards.front: Why was the Non-Cooperation Movement (1920) withdrawn?
cards.back: Due to the Chauri Chaura incident (5 Feb 1922) — a mob burned a police station killing 22 policemen in UP.

cards.front: INC was founded in which year and by whom?
cards.back: 1885 — by A.O. Hume (retired British civil servant) in Bombay. First president: W.C. Bonnerjee.

cards.front: 'Give me blood, I will give you freedom' — who said this?
cards.back: Subhash Chandra Bose — said to soldiers of the Indian National Army (INA) in 1944.

cards.front: Subhash Chandra Bose was born on which date and where?
cards.back: 23 January 1897, in Cuttack (present-day Odisha). His birth anniversary is celebrated as Parakram Diwas.

cards.front: What is 'Anandamath' and who wrote it? Why is it significant?
cards.back: Novel by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay (1882). The national song 'Vande Mataram' comes from this novel.

cards.front: The Lahore Session of INC (1929) is significant for what?
cards.back: Purna Swaraj (Complete Independence) was declared as the goal. Presided by Jawaharlal Nehru. 26 Jan chosen as Independence Day to be celebrated yearly.

cards.front: Who was Bagha Jatin and how did he die?
cards.back: Jatindra Nath Mukherjee — Bengali revolutionary. Died fighting British police in Balasore, Odisha in 1915 rather than surrender.

cards.front: Swami Vivekananda represented India at which event and in which year?
cards.back: Parliament of World's Religions, Chicago — 1893. His speech beginning 'Sisters and brothers of America' electrified the audience.

cards.front: What was the Gandhi-Irwin Pact (1931)?
cards.back: Agreement signed 5 March 1931 between Gandhi and Viceroy Lord Irwin. Gandhi suspended Civil Disobedience; British released political prisoners.

cards.front: Who founded the Brahmo Samaj and in which year?
cards.back: Raja Ram Mohan Roy — 1828 in Calcutta. He campaigned against Sati and for women's education.

cards.front: The Simon Commission (1927) was boycotted because?
cards.back: It had no Indian member — an all-British commission to review the Indian constitution. Lala Lajpat Rai died after a lathi charge during protests.

cards.front: What is the 'Lal-Bal-Pal' trio?
cards.back: Lala Lajpat Rai (Punjab), Bal Gangadhar Tilak (Maharashtra), Bipin Chandra Pal (Bengal) — the three extremist nationalist leaders.

cards.front: Matangini Hazra is known as?
cards.back: 'Gandhi Buri' — shot dead by police on 29 September 1942 in Tamluk while leading a procession and holding the national flag.

cards.front: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre — exact date, city, and general?
cards.back: 13 April 1919 (Baisakhi day), Amritsar. Ordered by General Reginald Dyer. Estimated 379+ killed officially (actual much higher).

cards.front: What was the INA (Indian National Army) and who formed it?
cards.back: Azad Hind Fauj — formed 1942 in Southeast Asia by Rash Behari Bose, reorganized and led by Subhash Chandra Bose from 1943.

cards.front: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar is known for which two major social reforms?
cards.back: 1. Widow Remarriage Act (1856) — got it legalized. 2. Women's education — established many girls' schools in Bengal.

cards.front: What was the Swadeshi Movement and what triggered it?
cards.back: Triggered by Partition of Bengal (1905). Movement to boycott British goods and use Indian-made products. Spread nationalism across India.

cards.front: Quit India Movement — exact date, place, and famous resolution?
cards.back: 8 August 1942, Gowalia Tank Maidan, Bombay. 'Do or Die' resolution. British arrested all major leaders the same night.

cards.front: Who founded the Ramakrishna Mission and in which year?
cards.back: Swami Vivekananda — 1897 in Belur Math, Howrah, West Bengal. Named after his guru Sri Ramakrishna Paramahansa.

cards.front: The Alipore Bomb Case (1908) involved which prominent Bengali revolutionary?
cards.back: Aurobindo Ghose (Sri Aurobindo). He was acquitted but later chose a spiritual path and settled in Pondicherry.

cards.front: 'Vande Mataram' — from which novel, by which author, first sung at which INC session?
cards.back: From Anandamath by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay. First sung at 1896 Calcutta Congress session by Rabindranath Tagore.
